description: In a living room, Joey stands balancing a long white object on his chin with his arms extended. Chandler stands behind him holding a notepad and pen. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey catches the object in his right hand and says, "Okay."
Joey turns and walks toward Chandler while holding the white object. Audience laughter continues.

Chandler looks at his notepad and says, "I'm going to be moving out. So you are going to be in charge of paying the rent." Joey responds, "Right. And when is that due?" Chandler answers, "First of the month."
Joey points the white object toward Chandler and asks, "That's every month?"
Chandler's gaze remains on Joey as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler looks back at his notepad and says, "No, just the months you actually want to live here." Joey keeps his gaze on Chandler.
Chandler turns a page on his notepad.
Chandler extends a piece of paper toward Joey and says, "Okay, here's the phone bill."
Joey takes the paper from Chandler and looks down at it.
Joey looks at the bill and says, "Oh, my God," with his mouth remaining slightly open afterward.
Chandler leans in to look at the paper Joey is holding.
Chandler points at a line on the paper and says, "That's our phone number."
Joey looks toward Chandler and smiles as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Look, I know I kind of sprung this whole me moving out on you thing. So why don't I just cover you for a—" Joey interrupts him and says, "no. No way."
Joey stands up from the couch and says, "Joey Tribbiani does not take charity," as he begins walking toward the kitchen.
Joey stops walking and turns back to face Chandler.
Joey says, "anymore," then turns his head back toward the kitchen area.
Audience laughter continues as Joey stands near the kitchen counter.

Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "It's not charity, Joe." Joey responds, "I don't know. Forget it, okay? I mean, thanks, but I'm done taking money from you. All right," with his arms outstretched.
Joey says, "I could take care of myself. Now, what's next?" Chandler looks at the papers and says, "Okay, here's the electric bill."
Joey looks at the paper and asks, "This is how much we pay for electric?" Chandler looks up and says, "Well, yeah."
Joey walks quickly toward the wall and flips a switch. The lights in the apartment turn off, and the scene goes dark. Loud audience laughter is audible.
From the darkness, Joey's voice asks, "So we'll do the rest of the bills later then?"
The room remains dark as audience laughter continues.

An upbeat instrumental song with vocals begins, featuring the lyrics "I'll be there for you." Multiple people dance in and around a fountain at night. On-screen text shows "FRIENDS" followed by various cast names such as "JENNIFER ANISTON" and "MATTHEW PERRY." Short clips of the characters in various indoor settings are shown. The song continues until the visual transitions back to an apartment living room.
Inside the living room, Phoebe walks across the floor holding a green bottle.

Phoebe stops and asks, "So is Joey going to have to give up the apartment?" Chandler, sitting on the floor by a cardboard box, says, "Well, I hope not. I tried to offer him some money, but he wouldn't take it."
Phoebe sits on the couch and asks, "Well, how much do you think he needs?" Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "I figured $1500 would cover him for a few months, you know?"
Chandler says, "But I have to trick him into taking it so I don't hurt his pride." Phoebe responds, "Well, why don't you hire him as an actor?"
Chandler puts his finger to his chin and looks away from Phoebe.
Phoebe gestures with her left hand and says, "You could have him dress up and put on little skits. Just whenever you want."
Chandler remains still as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler looks back at Phoebe and says, "Well, that would help the pride thing."
Monica enters the living room from the background, wearing a white button-down shirt and a red skirt.
Monica says, "Hey." Chandler turns and says, "Hey. Wow, you look great. Do you want to move in with me tomorrow?"
Monica smiles and says, "Okay."
Chandler smiles and says, "Okay."
Monica walks toward Chandler, and they embrace as audience laughter is heard.

Monica and Chandler are now seated together on the floor. Chandler asks, "So, what do you girls have planned for tonight?" Monica looks toward Phoebe and says, "Well, instead of being sad that tonight's my last night together with Rachel—"
Monica continues, "—we thought we'd go out to dinner and celebrate the fact that Rachel's moving in with Phoebe."
Phoebe lets out a short laugh.

Phoebe smiles and says, "And also my birthday." Monica turns to her and says, "It's not your birthday." Phoebe looks down at her bottle and says, "What a mean thing to say. I would never tell you it was not your—"
Phoebe drinks from the bottle. Ross and Joey enter the apartment through the front door.
Ross and Joey walk into the kitchen area. Joey says, "Hey," and Ross says, "Hi." Monica and Phoebe respond, "Hey."
Ross walks behind the counter while Joey continues into the living room.
Phoebe looks toward the men and asks, "So you guys have anything planned for the big last night?"
Chandler looks toward the kitchen and says, "Well, instead of just hanging out, we figured we'd do nothing."
Joey sits on the arm of the couch, looking toward Ross.
Ross holds a purple bottle and says, "Whoa."
Ross stands in the kitchen and says, "Knicks season opener tonight. I thought maybe you guys would come over and watch it." Joey is visible sitting on the couch.
Joey looks at Ross and says, "I don't know, Ross. Not if you're going to talk about how you gave up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist."
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "I did give up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist," his volume slightly louder than his previous line.
Ross remains standing in the kitchen as the audience laughter fades.
Inside a purple-walled apartment, Monica stands in the living area near the kitchen. A wooden door to the hallway is closed. Rachel opens the door.
Rachel steps inside, wearing a blue shirt and tan cargo pants. She looks toward Monica and says, "Pheebs. I was wondering why you're not dressed yet."
Monica gestures with her left hand and says, "We're supposed to start having fun in 15 minutes," her volume louder and at a higher pitch than her previous line.
Rachel stands still, keeping her gaze on Monica.
Rachel says, "Well, and clearly not a minute sooner," her vocal delivery flat.
Audience laughter is audible. Monica brings both hands to her chest.
Monica claps her hands together and asks, "You are packed, though, right? I mean, please tell me that you're packed."
Rachel extends both arms outward and says, "Of course I'm packed," her volume rising.
Rachel gestures with her right hand toward the side and says, "Monica, relax. Just wanted to ask Phoebe for her opinion on what I should wear tonight."
Phoebe, wearing a purple sweater and patterned pants, sits on the couch. She stands up and says, "My God, I cannot get a minute of peace," her volume rising.
Phoebe walks through a doorway into a pink-walled hallway and then into another room. Rachel follows closely behind.
Entering the room, Phoebe stops and says, "Oh, my God." The room is filled with piles of clothing on the bed and floor, along with boxes and packing materials.
Phoebe looks around at the clutter. Rachel stands in the doorway.
Phoebe turns her head toward Rachel and says, "Monica's just gonna kill you."
Rachel looks toward Phoebe and says, "Yeah, I know."
Rachel shifts her weight.
Phoebe gestures toward Rachel and says, "What you're wearing is fine for that."
Monica appears in the hallway behind Rachel and enters the room.
Monica looks at the cluttered bed and says, "You're not packed," her vocal delivery slow and volume low.
Monica steps closer and says, "You're not packed even a little bit," her volume louder than her previous line.
Rachel looks at Monica.
Rachel throws both arms out and shouts, "Surprise!" with a high pitch.
Monica stands still with her hands at her sides.
Rachel lowers one hand to Monica's shoulder and says, "What? Don't get mad, because look, this is what happened."
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "So I started packing, and then I realized, what am I doing? I am lousy at packing. Right?" Her volume rises on the final word.
Monica looks at Rachel.
Monica says, "But you love packing."
Rachel brings both hands to her chest and says, "So as a gift to you on our last night—"
Audience laughter is audible as Rachel pauses.
Rachel shouts "ta DA," and throws a large, white bag of packing material into Monica's arms.
Monica catches the bag and looks down at it. Rachel keeps her right arm raised.
Monica looks up and says, "I'll be coordinator," her volume rising with a high pitch.
Rachel keeps her gaze on Monica.
Monica looks at Rachel and says, "I'm sorry. I didn't get you anything."
Rachel smiles slightly.
Monica shifts the bag in her arms and says, "Okay, look, everybody has to help, okay?"
Monica turns her head toward Phoebe.
Monica asks, "Phoebe, you can help, can't you?" Phoebe, with her arms crossed, responds, "I have plans."
Monica asks, "You have plans with us?" Phoebe looks toward the side and says, "That's right."
Monica looks back at Rachel.
Monica gestures as she lists names. "All right. Chandler can make boxes. Ross can wrap and Joey can lift things. Now, Phoebe, go tell the guys they have to help out, okay?"
Phoebe says "Okay" and turns to leave. Rachel brings her hands together and says, "My God. Thank you."
Rachel and Monica hug. Phoebe exits the room and walks down the hallway.
Monica's voice is heard from the other room saying, "Hurry."

Phoebe enters the kitchen area where Joey, Chandler, and Ross are standing near the table. She says, "Monica's going to make you pack. She's got jobs for everyone. Now, it's too late for me, but save yourselves," her vocal delivery fast.
Joey, Chandler, and Ross turn and run toward the apartment's main door.
Phoebe adds, "okay?" as the men reach the door.

Monica enters the living area from the hallway. Phoebe turns toward her. Monica says, "The movers will be here in 11 hours. Rachel has not packed. Now, everybody has to help. Chandler, we're going to start."
Chandler, Ross, and Joey stand together. Chandler says, "No, I have plans for Joey."
Monica asks, "But you said you were going to do nothing."
Chandler looks at her and says, "Yes, but for the last time."
Monica keeps her hands clasped in front of her.
Monica says, "Okay, fine."
Monica turns to Ross and says, "Now, Ross."
Ross shrugs his shoulders and says, "Oh, but I can't do it." Monica asks, "Why not?"
Chandler asks, "Don't you have Ben?" Ross points his finger to his chest and says, "Because if I have Ben," his volume rising.
Ross looks toward Monica.
Ross says, "it's almost 08:00. It's almost past his bedtime," his volume rising on the final phrase.
Monica asks, "Where is he?"
Chandler looks at Ross and says, "He's at a dinner party," his vocal delivery flat.
Ross looks from Chandler to Monica and then toward the kitchen.
Monica gestures toward the window and asks, "Is he really coming? Because I can see into your apartment."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Of course he is. What? You think I just use my son as an excuse? What kind of father do you think I am?" His volume rises on each question.
Monica keeps her hands at her waist.
Monica says, "All right."
Ross says, "Sorry."
Ross turns and begins to walk toward the door.
Ross looks back at Monica and says, "I got to go make a fake Ben."

Upbeat music with a rhythmic guitar beat plays. The setting changes to a street view of Washington Square Park at night with cars driving past, then to the kitchen area of another apartment. Joey enters carrying two pizza boxes.

Joey walks toward Chandler and says, "Here it is. Our last pizzas together as roommates." Chandler gestures with his right hand and says, "I wish I'd known you were going to do that. I ordered Chinese." Joey lowers his head and says, "Oh, well, that's okay."
Joey looks back up at Chandler and says, "Hey, actually, in a way, it's kind of, you know, our last dinner together."
Joey gestures toward himself and then toward Chandler. "Me, bringing the food of my ancestors you, the food of yours."
Joey looks at Chandler and raises his eyebrows. Chandler stands by the counter, looking back at him. The upbeat music continues.

Inside an apartment kitchen, Chandler stands leaning against a wooden counter while Joey stands near a kitchen sink. Chandler wears a blue plaid shirt over a grey t-shirt; Joey wears a long-sleeved olive green shirt and blue jeans.

Chandler looks toward Joey and says, "Say, Joe, I had a strange idea for what we could do for our last night. What do you say we play a little foosball for money?" Joey holds a small dark object and looks back at Chandler, responding, "Are you crazy?"

Joey turns and walks toward a paper towel holder on the wall. He says, "You haven't beaten me once since my injury-plagued '97 season. Be easier if you just give me your money." Chandler, still leaning on the counter, replies, "Yes, it would." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey pulls a paper towel from the roll and turns back toward Chandler. Chandler looks down at the floor and then back up.
Chandler raises his hands slightly in front of him and asks, "What do you say to $50?"
Joey raises his right hand with his fingers spread and says, "I say to it $50."
Joey continues, his hand lowering slightly, "You are more than I have." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler shifts his gaze to the side briefly before looking back at Joey.
Chandler steps toward the foosball table and says, "If you know that you're going to win though, what difference does it make?"
Joey raises both arms out to his sides and says, "Okay, you're on." Chandler stands at one side of the table and says, "Okay, let's play the big game."
Chandler leans over the foosball table handles and says, "Italy versus China. Apparently." Audience laughter is audible.

Upbeat music with a rhythmic guitar beat begins. The setting changes to a bedroom filled with boxes and clothing. Rachel, wearing a blue t-shirt and khaki pants, stands holding a pair of black rollerblades.

Rachel holds up the rollerblades and says, "Oh, look. It's the roller blades." Monica, in a purple long-sleeved shirt, is on the floor kneeling by a box. Phoebe, in a purple sweater, stands behind her. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks up from the box toward Rachel.
Rachel steps forward, gesturing with the skates, and asks, "Remember when we got these?"
Monica keeps her gaze fixed on Rachel.
Monica says, "No," then looks back down at the box. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel tilts her head slightly while looking at Monica.
Rachel looks back at the skates and says, "I guess you weren't there."
Monica stands up and moves toward Rachel, putting her arms around her.
Monica and Rachel embrace. Phoebe stands behind them and says, "You guys, we said we were going to have fun. Come on. Hey, remember the time..."
Phoebe leans forward and clasps her hands together as she laughs. Monica and Rachel remain in an embrace, looking toward her. Audience laughter track overlaps Phoebe's laughter.
Phoebe stops laughing, looks at Rachel and Monica, and asks, "You don't remember?"
Monica and Rachel look at each other and then back toward Phoebe.
Rachel raises her right hand to her neck area and says, "I'm sorry, Pheebs. I guess I'm just really sad that I'm leaving. I'm going to miss you so much." Her volume is softer than her previous line.
Phoebe steps closer to the two women and says, "Well, this doesn't have to be so sad, though. You know?"
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "Maybe instead of just thinking about how much you're going to miss each other, you should think about some..."
Phoebe's hands move slightly as she finishes, "...of the things that you're not going to miss."
Monica and Rachel look at each other. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks back at Phoebe and says, "I don't think there's anything."
Phoebe raises her eyebrows and says, "Come on. There's got to be something."
Monica looks at Rachel and smiles.
Monica reaches out and touches Rachel's arm, saying, "She's perfect."
Rachel and Monica embrace again. Audience laughter track is audible.
Rachel pulls back from the hug and says, "I have one."
Phoebe looks between the two of them and nods her head.
Phoebe says, "Great."
Phoebe continues to look at Rachel.
Phoebe nods toward Rachel and says, "Okay, you can go first."
Rachel holds Monica's hands and says, "Well, I guess I'm not going to miss the fact that you're never allowed to move the phone pen." Her gaze remains on Monica.
Monica begins to laugh and claps her hands together. Phoebe joins in the laughter. Audience laughter track is audible.
Phoebe says, "That's a good one. Okay." She continues to laugh slightly.
Phoebe looks at Monica and gestures toward Rachel, saying, "Monica, anything? You know, does Rachel move the phone pen?"
Monica looks up and then back at Rachel.
Monica leans toward Rachel and says, "Oh, sometimes."
Rachel keeps her gaze on Monica.
Rachel looks at Monica and says, "Always, actually." Her volume is lower than her previous line.
Phoebe smiles while looking at the two women. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "Okay, good. There you go."
Phoebe looks between Monica and Rachel and asks, "Doesn't everyone feel better?"
Monica reaches into a wooden trunk and pulls out a picture frame.
Monica holds the frame up toward Rachel and says, "Not just the phone pen."
Rachel looks at the frame in Monica's hands.

Monica gestures toward the frame and says, "I never get my messages." Rachel responds, "You get your messages." Monica says, "Yeah, well, I don't think it really counts if you have to read them off the back of your..."
Monica gestures with her right hand and continues, "...hand after you fall asleep on the couch." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel crosses her arms over her chest.
Rachel looks at Monica and asks, "So you missed a message? From who? Chandler?"
Rachel continues, "Or your mom?" Her pitch rises on the final word.
Monica keeps her arms crossed and maintains her gaze on Rachel.
Rachel repeats, "Or Chandler or your mom?" Her pitch rises again at the end. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe looks at Rachel and Monica while they continue to look at each other with their arms crossed.
Phoebe looks toward the corner of the room and says, "Great, it worked. Now no one's sad."
Upbeat music with a rhythmic beat plays. The setting changes back to the kitchen where Chandler and Joey are at the foosball table.
Chandler stands at the foosball table handles.
Chandler raises both arms into the air and shouts, "Yes!"
Chandler says, "I win again," while gesturing toward the table. Joey looks down at the table.
Joey gestures with his right hand toward himself and then the table.
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "That's $500 you owe me."
Joey turns and walks away from the foosball table toward the refrigerator.
Joey opens the refrigerator door, looks inside, and says, "$500? That is a lot of electricity, huh?" Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stands with the refrigerator door open, looking at the shelves.
Joey looks back at Chandler and says, "I got to buy some food," before closing the refrigerator door.
Chandler remains standing on the opposite side of the foosball table.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Okay. Give me a chance to win my money back. Okay. Sudden death. One goal. $1,000."
Joey stands near the refrigerator and asks, "Are you serious?"
Chandler claps his hands together and says, "Oh, yes."
Joey gestures with his right hand and says, "Okay, get ready to owe me."
Chandler leans over the foosball table and says, "Okay, here we go."
Chandler looks at Joey and asks, "Ready?"
Chandler and Joey stand at opposite sides of the foosball table, their hands on the handles. The upbeat music continues.

Inside Joey and Chandler’s apartment, Joey leans over a foosball table while Chandler stands on the opposite side holding a white takeout box and chopsticks. Dramatic instrumental music plays. Joey keeps his eyes on the ball, his hands gripping the rods.

Joey quickly rotates one of the rods, striking the ball into the goal. Instrumental music stops, and audience laughter and cheering are audible. Joey keeps his gaze on the goal area, mouth slightly open.

Chandler lifts a container of Chinese food and uses chopsticks to put a bite in his mouth. On the foosball table, the ball rolls toward the center as the yellow and black player figures remain stationary. Audience laughter continues.
Joey looks down at the foosball table and says, "It."
The ball rolls across the green surface of the table, passing the "Tornado" logo. Joey remains leaned over the table, his gaze following the ball’s movement while Chandler continues to eat.
Joey continues to watch the ball, his head moving slightly as it travels between the rods. Chandler stands upright, chewing and holding his takeout container. Audience laughter is audible.

Joey stands up abruptly and points his right hand toward the table, then toward Chandler. He shouts "No!" repeatedly, his volume high and his pace rapid. Chandler’s mouth drops open, and he holds a single chopstick near his face.
Joey continues to point and shout "No!" while leaning slightly toward Chandler. Chandler stands still, his gaze fixed on Joey, his eyebrows slightly raised. Audience laughter continues.
The apartment setting remains the same. Joey stands with his arms crossed over his chest, his gaze directed toward the floor. Chandler reaches down and picks up the foosball from the table.

Chandler holds the takeout box in his left hand and a chopstick in his right. He says, "see, now that's why only the little fake men are supposed to do the kicking," his pace steady and his volume moderate. Joey remains standing with his arms crossed.

Chandler takes a bite of food. Joey continues to stand with his arms crossed, looking toward the foosball table. The scene changes to Monica’s living room, where boxes are piled on the floor. Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are carrying boxes. A telephone rings.

Monica holds a cordless phone to her ear and says, "Hello. Oh, hi, Ross. See, other people call me." Rachel, standing nearby and holding a box, raises one thumb and says, "OOH, your brother scored," her volume rising on "scored."
Monica keeps the phone to her ear and adjusts her grip on a small object in her other hand. She asks, "What's up?"
The setting changes to a living room where Ross stands near a window, holding a phone to his ear. He says, "What's up?"
Ross stands in a room with dark red walls and bookshelves. He says, "Oh, I'm just over here with Ben. Thought we'd say hi." He looks toward the side of the room while speaking.

Ross moves toward a couch and says, "Put him on. Ben. Say hi to Aunt Monica." He leans down and holds the phone toward a figure sitting on the couch that is wearing a striped sweater and a pumpkin mask.
Ross remains leaned over, holding the phone near the pumpkin mask. The figure does not move.
Ross pulls the phone back toward his own ear and says, "Oh, I guess he doesn't feel like talking right now," his volume slightly lower than before.
Ross keeps the phone to his ear, his gaze fixed on the pumpkin-masked figure. Audience laughter is audible.
Looking at the static pumpkin mask, Ross says, "He's smiling, though," his pace slow.
Ross remains in the same position, his gaze still on the figure. Audience laughter continues.
Ross says, "Okay, talk to you later," then lowers the phone from his ear.
Ross stands up and walks across the room toward a table. He sets the phone down and picks up a glass bottle. Upbeat instrumental music with a brass melody begins.
Ross walks back toward the couch and sits down next to the figure in the pumpkin mask. He exhales audibly and leans back against a pillow.
Ross holds the bottle and looks toward the pumpkin-masked figure. He says, "You know, Daddy was going to be a basketball player," his volume moderate. Audience laughter is audible.

The upbeat music continues. The setting changes to a bedroom filled with boxes and piles of clothes. Rachel and Monica walk into the room, each holding a glass of dark liquid. Phoebe sits on the floor near a wooden dresser.
Rachel stands in the middle of the room among the boxes and says, "I think it was better when you guys were sad." Monica stands nearby, looking toward a pile of clothes on the bed.
Monica turns toward a box and begins to move items. Phoebe remains seated on the floor, looking into a black bag.
Phoebe pulls a pair of black rollerblades from the bag, holds them up, and says, "Remember the roller blades?" her volume rising slightly.
Phoebe looks down at the rollerblades. Monica moves toward a stack of boxes. Rachel reaches for a blue garment on the bed.

Rachel holds the blue garment and says, "You know what else I'm not going to miss? I'm Monica. I wash the toilet 17 times a day, even if people are on it." She mimics a wiping motion against a windowpane. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stands with her hands on her hips, looking toward Rachel.
Monica pulls at the front of her own sweater and says, "Hi, I'm Rachel. Is my sweater too tight?" her volume higher and pitch rising on the question. Audience laughter continues.
Monica continues, "No. Oh, I better wash it and shrink it," while clutching her hands together near her chest.
Rachel stands still, looking toward Monica with her mouth slightly open.
Rachel says, "I'm Monica. I don't get phone messages from interesting people. Ever," emphasizing the word "ever." She keeps her gaze on Monica.
Phoebe, still seated on the floor with her glass, looks toward Monica and says, "Hey, call her."
Monica stands facing Rachel, her hands pressed together in front of her chest. She draws a breath.
Monica says, "Oh, my God. I love Ross," her voice rising in pitch and volume. She keeps her hands clasped near her neck and looks upward. Audience laughter is audible.
The bedroom scene remains visible for a moment before the screen fades to black. No further audio is heard.
